title: Chapter 13
text: Barbara Park says: “ We never had a Pet Day at my school, but since writing this book, I’ve tried to imagine what it would have been like. Not much fun for me, I don’t think. I had a big grouch of a cat named Pudgy. Picking Pudgy up was a bold move that only the bravest member of the family (my mother) would even attempt. On the nights Pudgy decided to sleep on my bed, I would carefully crawl under the covers and pray she’d let me sleep there, too. Pudgy was pure cat, through and through… independent, proud, and aloof. Pudgy is the reason I now have a dog. ”
